calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2017 Off Calendar

Notice And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Jun-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF SUCCESSFULMATCH.COM, INC.'S Motion To Compel Arbitration of its lawsuit against
defendant Virginia Sanderson is DENIED. The applicable law is set forth in Valencia v. Smyth (2010) 185 Cal. App. 4th 153, 177: "FAA's procedural provisions do not apply in state court unless the parties expressly adopt them'the question is not whether the parties adopted the CAA's procedural provisions: The state's procedural statutes ( 1281.2, 1290.2) apply by default because Congress intended the comparable FAA sections (9 U.S.C. 3, 4, 10, 11) to apply in federal court. The question, therefore, is whether the parties expressly incorporated the FAA's procedural provisions into their agreements." (Emphasis in original). Successfulmatch has cited no binding or persuasive authority stating that an arbitration provision that incorporates private arbitration rules by reference displaces California procedural rules, specifically CCP 1281.2(c). Sandquist v. Lebo Automotive, Inc. (2016) 1 Cal.5th 223, and Biomagic, Inc. v. Dutch Bros. Entertainers, LLC (C.D. Cal. 2010) 729 F.Supp.2d 1140, relied on by Successfulmatch in its supplemental memoranda, are not on point. Sandquist concerns the issue of who (the court or the arbitrator) decides an issue of contract interpretation that impacts the scope of the arbitrable issues. Nothing in Sandquist states or suggests that when, as here, a motion to compel arbitration is pending in a California court, the court should refrain from ruling on the application of section 1281.2(c) to the case or allow that issue to be decided by an arbitrator. Biomagic is a federal decision that explicitly chooses not to follow California law, which this court may not do. Section 1281.2(c) is a procedural rule. Section1281.2(c) applies to this motion because the Engagement Agreement between Successfulmatch and the Kronenberger firm does not expressly incorporate the procedural provisions of the FAA and because the arbitration agreement's language that the arbitrator "shall render an award in accordance with JAMS' Streamlined Arbitration Rules & Procedures" does not displace CCP 1281.2(c). Section 1281.2(c) is not a rule that addresses the rendering of an arbitration award and thus the quoted language cannot fairly be interpreted to preclude the application of that section. Because of the huge overlap between the factual and legal issues between this case and case 550648, this court exercises its discretion per section 1281.2(c) to deny Successfulmatch's motion to compel arbitration of this case. Denial of this motion - and the granting of the motion to consolidate the two cases - avoids the possibility of inconsistent rulings and fosters judicial economy. Order signed. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/hk

Notice And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-11-2017 Continued To Jun-08-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. WILLIAM MCGRANE OF THE MCGRANE PC, TEL. NO. 1-415-292-4807 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. WILLIAM L. COGGSHALL OF ARCHER NORRIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-925-639-8738 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Successfulmatch.com's motion to compel arbitration of its lawsuit against defendant Virginia Sanderson is continued to June 8, 2017 for briefing and argument on the sole issue whether the parties arbitration agreement by using the language in accordance with JAMS streamlined arbitration rules and procedures has displaced CA Procedure Rules, specifically 1281.2c of the code of civil procedure such that the statute is not applicable to these motions. Brief to be filed by May 19, 2017; Opposition to be filed by May 26, 2017; Reply to be filed by June 2, 2017. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/hk
